title: Percentage and Column Jumps
subtitle: {n}% jumps to a percentage of the file. {n}| jumps to a column.
summary: {n}% jumps to that percent through the file. {n}| jumps to column n on the current line. Both are obscure, both are occasionally exactly what you want.

blocks.text: {key:{n}}{key:%} is unusual: it jumps to {n} percent of the way through the file. {key:5}{key:0}{key:%} lands you in the middle of the buffer. Useful for big files when you don't want to count lines.
{key:{n}}{key:|} jumps to column {n} on the current line. Useful when working with fixed-width data, makefiles, or anything where columns matter.

blocks.label: Jump to column {n} of the current line

blocks.title: % Has Two Personalities
blocks.text:
{key:%} *without* a count is the **match-bracket** motion: jump from a `(` to its matching `)`, etc. See [`wider-motions.match-bracket`](#wider-motions.match-bracket).

{key:%} *with* a count is **percentage-of-file**. Vim picks the meaning based on whether a count was typed. So {key:%} = bracket; {key:5}{key:0}{key:%} = halfway down the file.

This kind of overloading is rare in Vim and worth noting — it's why {key:%} is a small surprise the first time you accidentally type a count before it.

rows: Jump to column {n} of current line
